description: Same doctor appointment with different description details - should match

content_parts.text:
You are comparing calendar events to detect duplicates.

**New Event:**
- Title: Dr. Smith - Annual Physical
- Start: 2026-04-22T10:30:00
- End: 2026-04-22T11:30:00
- Location: Riverside Medical Center, Suite 200
- Description: Appointment confirmation for annual physical exam. Please arrive 15 minutes early to complete paperwork.

**Existing Events (same date):**

1. Event ID: event-001
   - Title: Annual Physical Exam
   - Start: 2026-04-22T10:30:00
   - End: 2026-04-22T11:30:00
   - Location: Riverside Medical Center
   - Description: Dr. Smith - yearly checkup

2. Event ID: event-002
   - Title: Grocery Shopping
   - Start: 2026-04-22T14:00:00
   - End: 2026-04-22T15:00:00
   - Location: Whole Foods
   - Description: Weekly shopping

**Question:** Is the new event the same as any of the existing events?

Consider events the same if they refer to the same real-world event, even if:
- Wording is slightly different
- One has more details than the other
- Time changed slightly (updates)

Return the matching Event ID (or null if no match) and brief reasoning.

trace.raw_response_text: {"matched_event_id": "event-001", "reasoning": "The new event has the same start and end time (2026-04-22T10:30:00 to 2026-04-22T11:30:00) as event-001, and both refer to an annual physical exam at Riverside Medical Center (with the new event specifying Suite 200, which is a subset of the location). The titles and descriptions are consistent in meaning despite minor wording differences (e.g., 'Annual Physical Exam' vs 'Dr. Smith - Annual Physical', and 'yearly checkup' vs 'appointment confirmation for annual physical exam'). Event-002 is excluded due to a different time slot."}
